glossary.label: Abstraction Point
glossary.definition: Location where water is taken from the environment for use.

glossary.label: Calibration Event
glossary.definition: Documented activity to establish relation between instrument indications and standards.

glossary.label: Catchment
glossary.definition: Land area draining to a water body.

glossary.label: Discharge Point
glossary.definition: Location where effluent is released to a receiving water.

glossary.label: Instrument Platform
glossary.definition: Physical structure hosting sensors.

glossary.label: Method
glossary.definition: Specified analytical or sensing procedure used to produce results.

glossary.label: Monitoring Site
glossary.definition: Fixed feature designated for repeated environmental observations.

glossary.label: Observable Property
glossary.definition: A property that can be observed or measured by a sensor.

glossary.label: Platform
glossary.definition: A physical structure or system hosting one or more sensors.
glossary.notes: Core observation component in the sensing stack; links sensor deployment to monitoring sites and data streams.

glossary.label: Sensor
glossary.definition: System that implements a sensing method to estimate property values.

glossary.label: Telemetry System
glossary.definition: Digital system that transports sensor data to storage or analytics.

glossary.label: Virtual Site
glossary.definition: Conceptual monitoring location defined by coordinates/logic rather than physical hardware.

glossary.label: Water Body
glossary.definition: Hydrological feature managed as a reporting unit (river, lake, transitional, coastal).

glossary.label: Unit
glossary.definition: Reference unit in which a result is expressed.
glossary.notes: Essential for data interoperability and automated reasoning in analytics and modelling pipelines.

glossary.label: Determinands
glossary.definition: The substances or properties measured in water to indicate quality or condition.

glossary.label: pH
glossary.definition: Negative base-10 logarithm of hydrogen ion activity in water.

glossary.label: Turbidity
glossary.definition: Reduction of transparency due to light scattering by suspended material.

glossary.label: Water Temperature
glossary.definition: Thermodynamic temperature of water at observation time/place.

glossary.label: Aggregation Level
glossary.definition: Temporal aggregation applied to results (e.g., raw, hourly mean, daily mean).

glossary.label: Observation
glossary.definition: Act of estimating a property value of a feature using a procedure.

glossary.label: Phenomenon Time
glossary.definition: Time the measured property applies to the feature of interest.
glossary.notes: Often equals resultTime for instant readings.

glossary.label: QA Flag
glossary.definition: Qualifier indicating assessed validity state of a result.

glossary.label: Result Time
glossary.definition: Instant at which the observation result became available.

glossary.label: Result Value
glossary.definition: Numeric value assigned to the measurand by an observation.

glossary.label: Sampling Interval
glossary.definition: Elapsed time between successive measurements or logged values.

glossary.label: Unit of Measure
glossary.definition: Reference unit in which a result is expressed.

glossary.label: Validation Method
glossary.definition: Procedure or rule set used to assess plausibility or conformance of results.

glossary.label: Bathing Water Directive
glossary.definition: Standards and classes for bathing water microbiology.

glossary.label: Directive
glossary.definition: Formal regulatory instrument (e.g., EU Directive) specifying obligations.

glossary.label: Drinking Water Directive
glossary.definition: Standards for water intended for human consumption.

glossary.label: Environment Act Section 82
glossary.definition: UK legal requirement for continuous water quality monitoring near discharges.

glossary.label: Environmental Quality Standards
glossary.definition: Priority substance limits and compliance requirements.

glossary.label: Environmental Quality Standards Directive
glossary.definition: EU legislation establishing environmental quality standards for priority substances in water.
glossary.notes: Provides the legal framework for chemical status assessment and priority substance limits across surface and groundwater bodies.

glossary.label: Performance Commitment
glossary.definition: Regulator-set target (e.g., leakage reduction).

glossary.label: Regulation
glossary.definition: Law, directive, or policy defining monitoring and quality requirements.

glossary.label: Regulator
glossary.definition: Government body enforcing water laws and guidance.

glossary.label: Reporting Obligation
glossary.definition: Legally required data submission to regulators.

glossary.label: Section 82
glossary.definition: The UK legal requirement under the Environment Act 2021 for continuous water quality monitoring near discharge points.
glossary.notes: Central driver for current CWQM programmes; mandates monitoring design, determinand selection, and reporting obligations.

glossary.label: Urban Waste Water Treatment Directive
glossary.definition: Requirements for collection, treatment, discharge, and related monitoring.

glossary.label: Water Framework Directive
glossary.definition: Framework requiring water bodies to reach “good status.”

glossary.label: Jurisdiction
glossary.definition: Legal or geographic scope a threshold/regulation applies to.

glossary.label: River Type
glossary.definition: Typology factor (e.g., lowland/high alkalinity) influencing thresholds.

glossary.label: Statistic
glossary.definition: Summary statistic used by a threshold (e.g., mean, P90, P10).

glossary.label: Status Class
glossary.definition: Category showing how quality compares to standards (High/Good/Moderate/Poor/Bad).

glossary.label: Threshold
glossary.definition: Boundary values partitioning determinand results into status classes for a context.

thresholds.definition: The concentration of oxygen dissolved in water, indicating its capacity to support aerobic aquatic life.
thresholds.notes: Key indicator of aquatic ecosystem health.
thresholds.ontology_mapping: sosa:Observation → sosa:observedProperty DO → qudt:unit mg/L → hasThresholdSet → hasStatusClass
thresholds.ontology_notes: If using %sat, capture method & temp/sal correction.

thresholds.definition: The sum of un-ionised ammonia (NH₃) and ammonium (NH₄⁺) expressed as nitrogen; toxic to aquatic organisms.
thresholds.notes: Toxic at high levels; key indicator of sewage impact.
thresholds.ontology_notes: Toxicity pH/Temp dependent; record pH, Temp.

thresholds.definition: Dissolved reactive phosphate expressed as phosphorus; the primary inorganic form driving eutrophication.
thresholds.notes: Main nutrient driver of eutrophication.

thresholds.definition: Oxidised inorganic nitrogen (NO₃⁻) expressed as nitrogen; key nutrient indicating agricultural or wastewater inputs.
thresholds.notes: Elevated nitrate linked to agricultural runoff.

thresholds.definition: A logarithmic measure of hydrogen ion activity that indicates how acidic or alkaline the water is.
thresholds.notes: Outside this range stresses aquatic life.
thresholds.ontology_notes: Influences ammonia toxicity; no universal bands.

thresholds.definition: The thermodynamic temperature of the water at the point and time of observation.
thresholds.notes: High temperatures reduce oxygen solubility.

thresholds.definition: Optical clarity reduction caused by light scattering from suspended particles, reported in NTU/FNU.
thresholds.notes: Proxy for sediment load and disturbance.
thresholds.ontology_notes: Useful for event detection; not WFD driver.

thresholds.definition: Mass concentration of particulate matter retained by a specified filter from a well-mixed sample.
thresholds.notes: Excess TSS can smother benthic habitats.

thresholds.definition: Method-defined sum of dissolved and particulate nitrogen species (organic and inorganic).
thresholds.notes: Overall nutrient pressure indicator.

thresholds.definition: Method-defined sum of dissolved and particulate phosphorus species after digestion.
thresholds.notes: Key eutrophication driver alongside P-PO₄.

thresholds.definition: Dissolved fraction of copper in water (typically ≤0.45 μm filtered) relevant to chemical status assessment.
thresholds.notes: Toxic to fish and invertebrates at high levels.

thresholds.definition: Dissolved fraction of zinc in water (typically ≤0.45 μm filtered) used for chemical compliance checks.
thresholds.notes: Common pollutant from urban and industrial runoff.

thresholds.definition: Photosynthetic pigment concentration used as a proxy for algal biomass.
thresholds.notes: Indicator of algal biomass and eutrophication.

thresholds.definition: Electrical conductance of water (often normalised to 25 °C) indicating ionic strength/salinity.
thresholds.notes: Elevated conductivity indicates saline intrusion or pollution.

thresholds.definition: Faecal indicator bacteria enumerated as colony-forming units per 100 mL.
thresholds.notes: Microbial water quality indicator.
thresholds.ontology_notes: Increasing use for CSO impact checks.

thresholds.definition: Oxygen consumed by microbial decomposition over five days under specified conditions.
thresholds.notes: Organic pollution indicator.

thresholds.definition: Oxygen equivalent of chemically oxidisable substances measured by a specified oxidant method.
thresholds.notes: Overall organic load measure.

thresholds.definition: Concentration of phenolic compounds determined by a standard colorimetric or equivalent method.
thresholds.notes: Representative of industrial organic contamination.

thresholds.definition: Concentration of suspended particles measured from a single (grab) sample using a defined filtration/gravimetric method.
thresholds.notes: Single-point equivalent to TSS.

thresholds.definition: Broad indicator group of coliform bacteria enumerated as cfu per 100 mL to screen microbial contamination.
thresholds.notes: Indicator of faecal contamination.
thresholds.ontology_notes: Use with Enterococci for bathing waters.

thresholds.definition: Dissolved fraction of lead (≤0.45 μm) in water relevant to priority substance assessment.
thresholds.notes: Derived from EQS for freshwater ecosystems.

thresholds.definition: Total concentration of mercury in water; a bioaccumulative priority hazardous substance.
thresholds.notes: Bioaccumulative and highly toxic; extremely low EQS.
thresholds.ontology_notes: Often monitored via biota rather than water.

thresholds.definition: Dissolved fraction of nickel used in chemical status compliance against EQS values.
thresholds.notes: Industry-related heavy metal; toxicity to invertebrates.

thresholds.definition: Dissolved fraction of cadmium with hardness-tiered environmental quality standards.
thresholds.notes: Thresholds depend on water hardness; values shown are indicative.
thresholds.ontology_notes: Requires hardness class to choose band.

thresholds.definition: Total chromium concentration; screening parameter where hexavalent chromium (Cr VI) is of particular concern.
thresholds.notes: Chromium VI is particularly hazardous; total used for screening.

thresholds.definition: Dissolved arsenic concentration in water from natural or industrial sources.
thresholds.notes: Naturally occurring but also industrial contaminant.

thresholds.definition: Dissolved aluminium concentration influenced by pH and acidity; relevant to ecological effects.
thresholds.notes: Influenced by pH and water hardness.

thresholds.definition: Measure of extractable hydrocarbons and related compounds indicating petroleum contamination.
thresholds.notes: Indicative threshold for hydrocarbon pollution.

thresholds.definition: Aggregate measure of petroleum-derived hydrocarbons, often reported by carbon-range fractions.
thresholds.notes: Common indicator of fuel and oil contamination.

thresholds.definition: Count of synthetic polymer particles within a size range per unit volume of water.
thresholds.notes: Currently advisory; no statutory standard yet.
thresholds.ontology_notes: No statutory limits yet; method evolving.

thresholds.definition: Total cyanide species concentration (free and complexed) determined by a specified analytical method.
thresholds.notes: Highly toxic; rare but important industrial contaminant.

thresholds.definition: Index based on total coliform counts used as a general indicator of microbial water quality.
thresholds.notes: Indicator of overall microbial contamination.

thresholds.definition: Intestinal enterococci bacteria enumerated as cfu per 100 mL; key bathing-water indicator.
thresholds.notes: Microbial water quality parameter.

thresholds.definition: Number of distinct algal bloom events occurring within a defined period at a site.
thresholds.notes: Eutrophication indicator; not a direct EQS but relevant for risk.
thresholds.ontology_notes: Derived indicator; link to Chl-a & nutrients.

thresholds.definition: Biotic index scoring aquatic macroinvertebrate families to assess ecological quality.
thresholds.notes: Biological quality element for ecological status.

thresholds.definition: Biomass of planktonic algae, commonly proxied by chlorophyll-a concentration.
thresholds.notes: Proxy for nutrient enrichment.

thresholds.definition: Biotic index reflecting flow-regime suitability based on macroinvertebrate community composition.
thresholds.notes: Measures flow regime suitability.

thresholds.definition: Composite index assessing fish community status relative to reference conditions.
thresholds.notes: Biological quality element for ecological status.

thresholds.definition: Biomass of substrate-attached algae per unit area, indicating eutrophication pressure.
thresholds.notes: Algal growth indicator for substrate-attached communities.

thresholds.definition: Total duration of CSO discharge operation within a defined reporting period.
thresholds.notes: Operational indicator relevant to overflow impact.
thresholds.ontology_notes: Key operational KPI linked to Section 82 context.

section82: (Environment Act 2021, Part 5: Water, Section 82 – Continuous water quality monitoring near storm overflows and other discharges)
Sewerage undertakers (i.e. water and wastewater companies) operating in England.
Applies specifically to undertakers responsible for storm overflows, combined sewer outfalls (CSOs), or other relevant discharges into surface waters.
Section 82 creates four layers of legal obligation:
Monitoring duty – Continuous measurement of specified water quality determinands near discharge points.
Technical duty – Use of appropriate sensors, installation, calibration, and QA/QC standards.
Data duty – Structured data collection, retention, submission, and public publication.
Enforcement duty – Compliance monitored through permits, inspections, performance commitments, and public transparency.
